[A beautiful simile involving Slesa—pun is employed in this
verse couched in the w^rfcm^r metre. The Countries etc, that are
affected are mentioned in Chapters XIV and XV infra.

The last verse is patently an interpolation by a scholar
interested in electional astrology.
Another interpolated passage speaks of 105 Ketus, of which
10 are Varuna's children, 24 ofthe Sun, 18 of Kubera, 9 ofYama,
24 of Fire and 20 of Wind. When a child of Varuna rises (and
that is in the months of Sravana and Bhadrapada i.e. the rainy
